headline: The recommended educators converge on agentic AI as the dividing line between businesses that adopt fast and those that fall behind.
body: Cyril Imhof returns repeatedly to a widening adoption gap: AI agents like Claude now outperform RAG chatbots on file management, spreadsheet creation, and communications [1], while businesses that delay agentic deployment face a growing competitive gap defined by hardware and foundational building blocks [3][10]. He pairs this with infrastructure arguments, crediting OpenAI's early compute and data center investment for its breakthroughs [5], granting big tech a scale cost advantage [8], and predicting small open-source models run locally on consumer hardware within 18 months to 2 years, alongside a case for keeping sensitive Excel data on-premises rather than in Azure [4][7]. Claude formalizes the agent thread with an agent identity model that lets it act under its own scoped identity in Slack with admin governance and audit trails [13], and IBM Technology reframes security spending as strategic risk thinking that does not block innovation [9]. On the teaching side, Caleb Writes Code walks through GPT internals including self-attention, feed-forward networks, embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections [0], and AI Master demos no-code Claude plus NotebookLM content workflows [11].
